Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: fix Tesseract/Leptonica build failures on 3 targets

Three fixes for the remaining build-deps failures:

1. macOS x86_64: Tesseract 5.5.2 uses std::filesystem unconditionally,
   unavailable on our macOS 10.14 deployment target. Polyfill with
   ghc::filesystem v1.5.14 (header-only, MIT license) via sed-patch
   of baseapi.cpp and ccutil.cpp.

2. Windows (both targets): Tesseract links -lWs2_32 (mixed case) but
   MinGW sysroots have libws2_32.a (lowercase). Fix by removing the
   CLI executable build entirely — we only need the static library.

3. Windows (latent): Leptonica sets a versioned .dll suffix on MINGW
   even for static builds, producing libleptonica-1.87.0.dll instead
   of libleptonica.a. Guard with BUILD_SHARED_LIBS check.

Also remove redundant SED_I redefinition (already set in graphite2 section).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove Deno, pass --extractor-args directly to yt-dlp

Remove the Deno installation step from stream.yml — yt-dlp uses its
built-in jsinterp for n-sig decryption and does not need Deno.
Also removes the yt-dlp config file that was creating the extractor-args
via the Deno step.

Pass --extractor-args youtube:player_client=ios directly in main.js
so yt-dlp still requests iOS client formats (format 301) without
depending on an external Deno runtime.

FIX: Prevent race conditions in WebRTC message handling and room switching

Three fixes:

1. Serialize per-room MessageBus processing with a promise chain so
   async handlers (signals, participants, role changes) cannot interleave.

2. Block incoming signals during role changes — #handleSignal now checks
   #roleChangeInProgress, matching the existing guard in #handleParticipants.

3. Abort superseded joins when rapidly switching rooms. A join revision
   counter detects when a newer join has started, and the stale join bails
   out and sends a leave request to the backend. In-progress connecting
   rooms are also cleaned up immediately.

commit.message:
fix: use after() for self-chain to prevent stale jobs on Vercel

Root cause: fire-and-forget fetch() for self-chaining didn't reliably
execute on Vercel serverless — the runtime could kill the process before
the TCP connection was established, breaking the chain silently.

Fix: use next/server after() to guarantee self-chain fetch completes
after the response is sent. Also add defensive heartbeat update at the
start of each loop iteration, and fix speed calculation to use overall
job elapsed time instead of per-invocation time.

Add "Ask AI about the article" chat feature

After generating an AI summary, users can now open a chat window
to ask follow-up questions about the article. The AI answers based
on article content and flags when info isn't available. Supports
multi-turn conversation, Hebrew/English i18n, RTL, and mobile.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Transform static dashboard into full SaaS product

- Auth: Supabase sign up / sign in (auth.html) with Arabic UI, email
  confirmation handling, and post-login redirect logic
- Connect: WhatsApp QR onboarding (connect.html) via server-side Whapi
  proxy to avoid CORS; polls channel status every 5s until connected
- Dashboard: live dashboard (dashboard.html) pulling real data from
  Supabase with real-time subscriptions; replaces all hardcoded HTML
- Webhook: Vercel serverless function (api/webhook.js) receives Whapi
  message events, calculates SLA response times (30 min target), and
  stores messages / response_events / group stats in Supabase
- Channel API: server-side proxy (api/channel.js) for Whapi channel
  creation and QR polling
- Admin: protected admin dashboard (admin.html + api/admin.js) at /admin,
  gated by ADMIN_EMAIL env var; shows all clients, WhatsApp channel

commit.message:
fix: check for YouTube auth cookies, not just any cookies

The cookie readiness check was using !!cookies which returned true
for anonymous tracking cookies (VISITOR_INFO, PREF). This meant the
"Log into YouTube" card never appeared, and the backend received
useless anonymous cookies that can't bypass bot detection.

Now checks for actual auth cookies (SID, SSID, HSID, LOGIN_INFO,
__Secure-1PSID, __Secure-3PSID). If user isn't logged into YouTube:
- Shows "Log into YouTube" card with Open YouTube button
- User logs in → clicks refresh → auth cookies detected → input shows

Changes:
- extension/background.js: formatYouTubeCookies() returns isAuthenticated
- extension/webapp-bridge.js: passes isAuthenticated to webapp
- frontend useExtensionDetection: checks isAuthenticated not !!cookies

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
